scenario: Tessera Hospital schedules 4500 tasks per day in Camunda 8.8 across three regional sites and a developer needs to extract both the offset duration and the timezone identifier from a zoned date-time variable inside a FEEL expression on an exclusive gateway to choose the right escalation flow.
question: Which property combination correctly reads the offset duration and the timezone identifier from a zoned date-time value in FEEL?

options.text: The hour and minute properties together return the offset as a duration and the timezone as a string in a single tuple at evaluation time
The timezone property returns the offset duration value and the time offset property returns the timezone identifier string at evaluation time
The time offset property returns the offset duration such as duration("PT2H") and the timezone property returns the identifier such as "Europe/Berlin"
The day and weekday properties combine to expose the timezone identifier, while the offset is read from a separate date property at evaluation time

a.text: Incorrect. The hour and minute properties expose components of the time within the day, not the timezone or offset; the documented properties for that purpose are time offset and timezone.

b.text: Incorrect. The two properties are swapped in this option: time offset returns the offset duration and timezone returns the identifier, as the properties table documents on time and date-time values.

c.text: Correct. The Properties table maps time offset to the duration offset such as duration("PT2H") and timezone to the identifier such as "Europe/Berlin", available on time and date-time values.

d.text: Incorrect. The day and weekday properties expose calendar fields and have no relationship to timezone information; FEEL exposes timezone metadata only through time offset and timezone.

explanation: The Properties table on the FEEL temporal expressions page lists time offset (the duration offset corresponding to the timezone or null) and timezone (the timezone identifier or null) as the two properties for reading zone metadata from a time or date-time value.
